ai_summary: Elon Musk reports that a SpaceX Falcon 9 booster has completed its 34th flight, demonstrating the company's progress toward extreme rocket reusability and cost reduction. This milestone illustrates SpaceX's core business strategy of achieving rapid, repeated launches with the same hardware—a key driver of lower launch costs and increased revenue per asset, fundamentally reshaping the commercial spaceflight economics.
embed_html:
34th flight of this booster pic.twitter.com/sM4lQFTGA6

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) March 31, 2026

text:
Tesla is making a big investment in Japan with service & Superchargers. 

Many of the parts in Teslas are made in Japan. Panasonic has been our biggest strategic supplier over the past 2 decades. https://t.co/XkwdUIPeKy
summary: Tesla is making a big investment in Japan with service & Superchargers. Many of the parts in Teslas are made in Japan. Panasonic has been our biggest strategic supplier over the past 2 decades.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces Tesla's major investment in Japan focused on expanding service centers and Supercharger infrastructure, while highlighting the critical role of Japanese suppliers—particularly Panasonic—in Tesla's manufacturing ecosystem over the past two decades. The post illustrates a key business strategy: securing and strengthening relationships with strategic suppliers in critical markets, and using local infrastructure investment to deepen ties with both suppliers and customers in important geographies.

summary: 34th launch & landing of the same rocket
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced SpaceX's 34th successful launch and landing of the same Falcon 9 rocket, demonstrating a major milestone in rocket reusability. This represents a critical business achievement in reducing launch costs through rocket reusability—a core strategy for SpaceX to make space access more affordable and frequent, directly impacting the economics of satellite deployment and space infrastructure.

summary: Neuralink enables people with ALS to speak again
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced that Neuralink has successfully enabled an ALS patient to speak again, demonstrating a major breakthrough in the company's brain-computer interface technology. text:
Indeed, it was *because* I was not from the aerospace industry that SpaceX made such radical breakthroughs. Same for Tesla. 

Those in the industry would have if they could have. https://t.co/MYzMLrpOhh
summary: Indeed, it was *because* I was not from the aerospace industry that SpaceX made such radical breakthroughs. Same for Tesla. Those in the industry would have if they could have.
ai_summary: Elon argues that SpaceX and Tesla achieved radical breakthroughs precisely because he lacked aerospace and automotive industry experience, allowing him to pursue unconventional approaches that established players either couldn't or wouldn't attempt. The insight is directly applicable to entrepreneurs: outsider perspective and freedom from industry orthodoxy can be a competitive advantage when building transformative companies, as incumbents are often constrained by existing mental models and vested interests.
